Friend,

For the first time in a decade, we have a president in the White House who’s working for you.

You’ve already seen the benefits of that in a big way with tax reform.

But to keep these wins coming, we need senators in Washington who are going to fight alongside him.

[**That’s why today, I’m officially announcing my support for Leah Vukmir for Senate, and asking you to join me by chipping in $25 today.**](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=509e3a74e4da4647a47e780deade5b9f&_e=R3YPG4m4skQ2gD7p1tX_nVprYeJY-ypSHpTnD27OQMbB-WqOJwqn9acCuIodc8DwqQOae0ekR0lo-OI_Xo18ky2w7XYsKkyV76CGUW8VLQpbtacfdNABjbDRkmn4QBeEVJne6zyIC07WgC76FOEO9RGE6fsy_Cgc3MA4a0RvPhfETC303HAftli81mqQf7RdJNq2nnI0_IFUVKx5TD6dP7mr3fV5SVVkvpm1sKyCieEbkNv-EWOP9mbA73dp-KQJl4EqCN4AdjnbLUaedGrsO1xmXu-IQpbrfD9H8bFwWzLqZo19lfXmgwwX4Q7Iq9ajzdfSJVL99xIE1fSVceBFC7MBe29Ouxe-rW4El9gTD52P3nz7rKyaxgLpUmWARZQtEjdyxHIQ6uTb5MGJxGB8m6mrn-MNKkDGcRNG8k7KjkBrnesfwi3LdpAoRo-1YO4s)

Leah's work in the state Senate alongside Governor Scott Walker transformed Wisconsin when they said it couldn't be done. That's the kind of fight we need more of in Washington, aiding President Trump’s implementation of the America First agenda that WE voted for.

There’s no question about it, friend, we need Leah.

name: NHGOP Press & Communications
subject: Forrester Calls on Shaheen, Hassan, Kuster, & Shea-Porter to Withhold their Salaries
body:
**Forrester Calls on Shaheen, Hassan, Kuster, & Shea-Porter to Withhold their Salaries**

**For Immediate Release:** 1/22/2018
**Contact:** Patrick Hynes, (202) 321-5310
CONCORD - NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following statement regarding our Federal Delegation failing to avert a government shutdown:
"Senator Shaheen, Senator Hassan, Congresswoman Kuster, and Congresswoman Shea-Porter voted in lock-step with the Democrats, putting party politics over the interests of Granite Staters. As a result of their reckless and self-serving vote, they have jeopardized the well-being of 14,727 New Hampshire children who rely on the Children’s Health Insurance Program and put at risk our veterans and active military pay and benefits."
"And, while Shaheen, Hassan, Kuster, and Shea-Porter will still be bringing home a paycheck, the 7,600 federal employees in New Hampshire are going unpaid for the foreseeable future. Sadly, our delegation is once again out of touch and out of line and they should forgo their salary until the end of the shutdown."
###
**BACKGROUND**

name: Our Revolution
subject: Join Senator Bernie Sanders this Tuesday!
body:
#
Friends,
Think back to May of 2015 when Senator Bernie Sanders announced that he was going to run for president. Think about what the prospects were for Medicare for All in the United States. Now, fast forward to 2017 when Bernie's most recent Medicare for All bill had 16 Senate co-sponsors and overwhelming support across the country.
In just two years, Bernie has successfully shifted the national conversation about universal healthcare. Representatives and Senators in office are saying that healthcare is a human right. The fight for Medicare for All seemed impossible two years ago, and now, it is within our grasp.
**Can you make Bernie 's Medicare for All National Town Hall on Tuesday, January 23, and be a part of the growing momentum that is changing our country's healthcare systems?**
[**Medicare for All National Town Hall**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad5f/bed43eb/32743032/75feb2f2/610407419/VEsH/ "https://www.facebook.com/events/1003320739806341/")
Tuesday, January 23 at 7 p.m. EST
The United States Capitol Visitor Center
Bernie is circumventing the media corporations who refuse to cover the issues that real people care about by hosting a National Town Hall on the internet for anyone to see. Next Tuesday, Bernie will be speaking directly to us, the people.
When you want to change the laws of a town, you host a town hall and bring people together, talk about ideas, and forge compromise. When you want to change the country, you do the same thing. Bringing people together is how Bernie affected change as Mayor of Burlington, Vermont and how he has affected change across the country as a national leader.
[**The momentum is growing, speed is picking up, and we need you to be a part of this rapid change.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad5f/bed43eb/32743032/75feb2f2/610407419/VEsE/ "https://www.facebook.com/events/1003320739806341/")
****[RSVP](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad5f/bed43eb/32743032/75feb2f2/610407419/VEsF/ "https://www.facebook.com/events/1003320739806341/")****
In solidarity,
The Team at Our Revolution

603 2ND ST, NE
WASHINGTON, DC, 20002

name: NHGOP Press & Communications
subject: Forrester Statement on Shaheen and Hassan caving on the Government Shutdown
body:
**Forrester Statement on Shaheen and Hassan Caving on the Government Shutdown**

**For Immediate Release:** 1/22/2018
**Contact:** Patrick Hynes, (202) 321-5310
CONCORD - NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following statement regarding the end of the Government Shutdown:
“I am very happy the children of New Hampshire and the fighting men and women of our Armed Service can breathe a sigh of relief now that the shutdown is over. But what was the point of the Shaheen, Hassan, Kuster and Shea-Porter shutdown? It appears as though our federal delegation is strictly interested in playing political games. And once they realized the public was outraged with their shutdown shenanigans, they caved as soon as they could.”
###

name: Ellen R. Malcolm, EMILY's List
subject: 45 years of Roe v. Wade
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6bea/24c69fd9/131c350ee/1895ec1c/465883843/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFEM38N_digital_email_20180122_RoevWade_inact&source=WQEFEM38N_digital_email_20180122_RoevWade_inact")
---

Peter,

45 years ago today, the Supreme Court ruled in _Roe v. Wade_ that women have the right to access safe, legal abortion.

12 years later, in 1985, I sat down with a small group of feminist friends to try to find a way to put real money behind women candidates who would defend this decision and work to improve the lives of women and families.

Thus, EMILY's List and our mission of electing pro-choice Democratic women was born. And I'm proud that we've never strayed from exclusively supporting women who will defend _Roe_.

With a woman's right to choose under attack from the White House to Congress to state legislatures, our mission is still vital.

**[Donate $3 today so we can continue supporting women candidates who will _always_ defend _Roe_.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6bea/24c69fd9/131c350ee/1895ec1d/465883843/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180122_email_RoevWade?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+today+so+we+can+continue+suppor&utm_campaign=WQEFEM38N_digital_email_20180122_RoevWade_inact&source=WQEFEM38N_digital_email_20180122_RoevWade_inact")**

When we started EMILY's List, we debated if we should consider supporting anti-choice women candidates. And it's a position I still get questions about to this very day.

_Do you really have to support abortion? Is it really that important?_

Absolutely.

It's ludicrous to argue that women should be equal participants in our society but that we don't get to control our own bodies.

After 45 years, women controlling their own bodies shouldn't be up for debate. But with Donald Trump having the power to stack the courts with anti-choice judges, the right to choose is in serious danger.

And Republican legislators are now going even farther. They're mounting attacks on birth control, preventive health care services for women, and more. It infuriates me... and makes me more determined to fight back!

So we have to make sure we flip the Senate -- which reviews Trump's judicial nominees -- this year. And we need to replace anti-choice Republicans who are pushing all these anti-woman legislative attacks, too.

**Mark the anniversary of _Roe v. Wade_ by donating $3 today so we can continue supporting women candidates who will defend choice:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Roe-v-Wade](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6bea/24c69fd9/131c350ee/1895ec1e/465883843/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180122_email_RoevWade?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgRoevWade&utm_campaign=WQEFEM38N_digital_email_20180122_RoevWade_inact&source=WQEFEM38N_digital_email_20180122_RoevWade_inact")**

Thank you,

Ellen R. Malcolm
Founder and Chair of the Board, EMILY's List

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: How we win
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders
body:
#
Sisters and Brothers -
Over the last week we have seen the devastating impact of the right-wing extremist agenda that Republican rule in Washington is forcing on the nation. Over 80 percent of Americans support providing legal protection for the 800,000 Dreamers - young people brought to this country as children who are now our neighbors, our co-workers, our friends and our family members.
Despite the overwhelming view of the American people, Donald Trump, Mitch McConnell and Paul Ryan chose to hold the entire federal government hostage to their blatantly xenophobic and anti-immigrant views.
As I traveled the country in 2015 and 2016 some of the most moving meetings I held were with young Dreamers terrified that they would be rounded up and thrown out of the only country they have ever known. Trump promised to help the Dreamers while he was running for president but, in one of his series of lies, he has reneged on that pledge.
Make no mistake about it. This government shutdown and budget fight in Washington is also about our priorities as a nation. Republicans want massive increases in military spending while refusing to address the crises we face in health care, education, college affordability, infrastructure and more.
This is the wealthiest nation in the history of the world. Of course we can increase the standard of living of people in every zip code in this country - urban and rural - if we invest in our critical domestic needs. But we cannot expect that will happen when we have a president and a party beholden to the Koch brothers and other billionaire campaign contributors. Their agenda is exactly opposite of what the American people want.
**From coast to coast Americans want a government that represents all of us, not the 1 percent. They don't want congressional leadership that has tried repeatedly to throw tens of millions of people off their health insurance and then looted the U.S. Treasury to give massive tax breaks to their fat cat donors. This right-wing Republican leadership has got to go! And we are the ones who have to send them packing.**
But I cannot do it alone:
**[Chip in $3 to help us defeat Republicans across the country by reactivating the organizing networks that were at the heart of our grassroots success in 2015 and 2016 and to do it in support of progressive candidates up and down the ballot who will stand with Dreamers and the working people of this country.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/we-must-win?refcode=em180122-full "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/we-must-win?refcode=em180122-full")**
I, frankly, am outraged by what Trump and Washington Republicans are doing to our country and to our people. That is why it is so important that we take back the Congress in 2018 from these extremists and why I hope you will join me in that fight.
Being outraged, however, is not enough. We are the ones who have to make it happen. If we fail, what will you tell future generations when they ask, "What did you do in that critical moment when we still had a chance to set things right?"
How we win is straightforward. We have all witnessed the power of grassroots politics. Whether it was the 2016 primary campaign, the millions of women who took to the streets, the people from all walks of life who showed up at airports to protest the bigoted Trump Muslim ban, or the overwhelming majority standing with our Dreamer brothers and sisters, the message is clear. When we stand together we can build the America we all want to see.
Grassroots efforts have been successful in electing new progressive voices from all walks of life in places like Virginia, New York, Alabama, Mississippi and elsewhere. Grassroots efforts can transform this country. But we have to redouble our efforts if we are going to wrest our country back from those whose greed and dishonesty is destroying it.
That is why I want to announce to you a major new three-part initiative for this year and how you can be part of the fight to take our country back.
**First, I am committing to travel the country in support of progressives running up and down the ballot.** Like I did when together we rallied millions against the Republicans' failed attempt to repeal the Affordable Care Act, I intend to go to every corner of this country to help build the electoral wave that will sweep Republicans out of the Congress, out of governors' mansions, out of state houses and out of city and town halls.
I hope in my travels that I will see you at these events. Your attendance is critically important to showing the strength of our movement and our resolve.
**Second, as we work to create Democratic majorities in both houses of Congress, I will be giving special focus to a core group of up-and-coming progressive candidates at the national and state level.** We need progressive Democrats who are going to vigorously stand up against Republicans and fight for our values. But the only way they win is if we help them.
These are the type of candidates who do not draw big dollar contributions but they can prevail if we provide them the small dollar, grassroots support they need. As we introduce this growing list of candidates to you over the next couple of months I hope you will give them the support they need to carry our fight forward in their primaries and in the general election.
**Finally, I am excited to announce that we will be reactivating the distributed organizing network that was at the heart of our grassroots success in 2015/2016.** This will give us the capacity to reach out to millions of households by phone, by email, on social media and by text message.
This will give each of you the power to help elect progressives and defeat Republicans all across the country.
What we have seen over the last depressing year is a full out assault on working people of all races, an assault on marginalized communities and an assault on people based on gender, race, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity and national origin.
Trump and the Republicans want to divide and conquer while the billionaire class laughs all the way to the bank and the rest of America falls further and further behind. We cannot let them do it.
The late Congresswoman Barbara Jordan summed it up so well when she described the aspiration of everyone in this country - "What the people want is very simple - they want an America as good as its promise."
Creating that America has been the motivation of my entire political life. I have always believed that together we could make it a reality. Even in these dire times, I feel it more strongly than ever.
Join me in the fight!
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders

Over 80 percent of Americans support providing legal protection for the 800,000 Dreamers - young people brought to this country as children who are now our neighbors, our co-workers, our friends and our family members. Despite the overwhelming view of the American people, Donald Trump, Mitch McConnell and Paul Ryan chose to hold the entire federal government hostage to their blatantly xenophobic and anti-immigrant views. body:
| |
---
| |
---
| Today is the 45th anniversary of the Supreme Court's decision in _Roe vs. Wade_ , you know. Last week, Debbie asked us to gather 4,500 in support of a women's right to choose before this anniversary. **We're almost there -- with 4,194 signatures in and only 306 to go.** But there are only enough 5 hours left in the day. [_**Before midnight, help us reach this goal with room to spare. Add your name now.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113fb0/622b7b5b/191993f6/307bb889/3908975064/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180122") And if you missed Debbie's email, you can see it below. Thanks! Team DWS \---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
Subject: January 22nd On January 22, 1973, the Supreme Court issued its ruling in a landmark case. They upheld a woman's right to privacy in making decisions about her body and health. They said that the "right of privacy...is broad enough to encompass a woman's decision whether or not to terminate her pregnancy." And 45 years later, we're still fighting for that right. | [](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113fb0/622b7b5b/191993f6/307bb889/3908975064/VEsE/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180122")
---
There's a misogynist in the White House. Congress is under the control of Republicans who repeatedly and unabashedly attack women's healthcare access. **This is a critical time for women's rights -- a time when we cannot afford to back down.**
According to estimates, last year's Women's March in Washington, D.C. attracted even more attendees than President Trump's inauguration. People marched across the country and around the world.
This year, we're doing it again. As of today, there are 673 marches planned, which are expected to draw almost 5 million marchers.
But not everyone can make it to a march. That's why I wanted to give all of you the opportunity to show your solidarity, whether you plan on marching or not.
[_**By the anniversary of _Roe v. Wade_ on the 22nd, I want to gather 4,500 signatures in support of a woman's right to choose. Add yours >>**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113fb0/622b7b5b/191993f6/307bb889/3908975064/VEsF/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180122")
We can't let women's rights in this country go backward. If we're not careful, President Trump and Republicans in Congress will erode women's right to choose, little by little, until it's whittled down to nothing.
We cannot let that happen. I know fighting these same battles over and over again may get tiresome. But we can't afford to fall asleep on the job of protecting our right to choose.
[_**We have 5 days to gather 4,500 signatures. I know we can do it. Add your name to mine.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113fb0/622b7b5b/191993f6/307bb889/3908975064/VEsC/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180122")
Thank you.
Debbie
